title: "Opaline Dagger Puzzle v19531.71"

  description: "At 21:50, the Opaline Dagger vanished from a locked display in the Eastern Hall. Four individuals (1, 2, 3, 4) had valid access at that time. Each suspect gives three statements, all of which appear \emph{truthful} at face value. Yet precisely one suspect is responsible for the theft. Using only their statements—none of which explicitly contradict the others—you must find the single culprit, deducing how one suspect’s timeline or remarks subtly reveal guilt.

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'I visited the Eastern Hall at 21:45, briefly checking the display but leaving it locked.'
      (2) 'Suspect 2 asked me about the alarm system around 21:46.'
      (3) 'Suspect 4 and I parted ways at 21:48 near the main corridor.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) 'I arrived at the Eastern Hall exactly at 21:46, looking for maintenance logs.'
      (2) 'Suspect 1 walked away just as I came in, but the display was still secure.'
      (3) 'Suspect 3 asked me about corridor lights at 21:47, so I directed them to the control room.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I never touched that display after 21:40—I dislike tampering with locked cases.'
      (2) 'Suspect 2 was rummaging through files near 21:47, so I briefly asked them about the lighting.'
      (3) 'I glimpsed Suspect 4 standing near the exit door at 21:49.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) 'I finished a short corridor patrol at 21:48, then noticed Suspect 1 heading to the main hallway.'
      (2) 'Suspect 3 passed by me around 21:49, asking if I’d seen any suspicious activity.'
      (3) 'I am certain the display was intact when I last glanced at 21:49, just before stepping outside.'

  question: "Which suspect stole the Opaline Dagger? Provide a single digit (1,2,3,4). All statements are seemingly truthful, yet one subtle mismatch indicates who is guilty."
